name: Web3.Ui.Address
comment:
Address display and input components.

    -- Display with optional explorer link:
    Web3.Ui.Address.view []
        { explorerUrl = Just "https://etherscan.io/address/" }
        addr

    -- Truncated address string (pure, no Html):
    Web3.Ui.Address.short addr  --> "0x1234…abcd"

    -- Truncated with custom lengths (e.g. token detail page):
    Web3.Ui.Address.shortWith { prefixChars = 8, suffixChars = 6 } addr
    --> "0x123456…789abc"

    -- Address text input:
    Web3.Ui.Address.input []
        { value = model.addressInput
        , onInput = AddressInputChanged
        , valid = True
        }

@docs view, short, shortWith, input

values.name: input
values.comment:
A text input for address entry. Adds `web3-input-address--invalid` class
when `valid` is `False`.

The host app is responsible for validating with `T.address`.

CSS class: `web3-input-address` (plus `web3-input-address--invalid` when invalid)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : String.String, onInput : String.String -> msg, valid : Basics.Bool } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: short
values.comment:
Truncate an address to "0x" + first 4 hex chars + "…" + last 4 hex chars.

    short addr  --> "0x1234…abcd"
values.type: Web3.Types.Address -> String.String

values.name: shortWith
values.comment:
Truncate an address with configurable prefix and suffix lengths.

`prefixChars` is the total number of leading characters including `"0x"`.
`suffixChars` is the number of trailing hex characters.

    shortWith { prefixChars = 6, suffixChars = 4 } addr
    --> "0x1234…abcd"   (same as short)

    shortWith { prefixChars = 8, suffixChars = 6 } addr
    --> "0x123456…789abc"
values.type: { prefixChars : Basics.Int, suffixChars : Basics.Int } -> Web3.Types.Address -> String.String

values.name: view
values.comment:
Display an address as "0x1234…abcd". Optionally wraps in an `<a>` tag
when `explorerUrl` is `Just url`.

CSS class: `web3-address`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { explorerUrl : Maybe.Maybe String.String } -> Web3.Types.Address -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.Amount
comment:
Token amount input and display with SI suffix formatting.

    -- Amount input with inline symbol label:
    Web3.Ui.Amount.amountInput []
        { value = model.amountStr
        , onInput = AmountChanged
        , decimals = 18
        , symbol = "PLS"
        , valid = True
        }

    -- Format a Wei value as human-readable (caller appends symbol):
    Web3.Ui.Amount.formatWei 18 weiAmount ++ " PLS"
    --> "1.23M PLS"

@docs amountInput, formatWei

values.name: amountInput
values.comment:
Numeric text input for a token amount. `value` is a plain decimal string
(e.g. `"1000.5"`). No parsing is done here — call `Web3.Units.parseUnits` in
your `update` to convert to Wei.

Adds `web3-amount-input--invalid` when `valid` is `False`.

CSS classes: `web3-amount-wrapper` (outer div), `web3-amount-input` (input),
`web3-amount-symbol` (symbol label)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : String.String, onInput : String.String -> msg, decimals : Basics.Int, symbol : String.String, valid : Basics.Bool } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: formatWei
values.comment:
Format a Wei BigInt as a human-readable string with SI suffix.

The symbol is not appended — concatenate it yourself so the caller controls
spacing and placement.

    formatWei 18 onePls   --> "1"
    formatWei 18 largePls --> "1.23M"
    formatWei 6  oneUsdc  --> "1"

SI suffixes: K (10³), M (10⁶), B (10⁹), T (10¹²).
Values below 1000 are shown with up to 2 decimal places, trailing zeros trimmed.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> String.String

name: Web3.Ui.Balance
comment:
Balance display components.

Uses `Web3.Units.formatUnits` / `Web3.Units.formatEther` internally.

    -- ERC-20 token with 6 decimals:
    Web3.Ui.Balance.view []
        { decimals = 6, symbol = "USDC" }
        amount

    -- Native ETH/PLS balance:
    Web3.Ui.Balance.viewEther []
        { symbol = "ETH" }
        weiAmount

    -- Balance that may not have loaded yet:
    Web3.Ui.Balance.viewMaybe []
        { decimals = 6, symbol = "USDC", loading = "…" }
        model.balance

    -- Ether balance that may not have loaded yet:
    Web3.Ui.Balance.viewEtherMaybe []
        { symbol = "ETH", loading = "…" }
        model.ethBalance

@docs view, viewEther, viewMaybe, viewEtherMaybe

values.name: view
values.comment: Display a token balance formatted with the given decimal count and symbol. view [] { decimals = 6, symbol = "USDC" } amount --> 1.5 USDC CSS class: `web3-balance`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { decimals : Basics.Int, symbol : String.String } -> Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewEther
values.comment: Display a Wei amount as ether (18 decimals) with the given symbol. viewEther [] { symbol = "ETH" } weiAmount --> 1.5 ETH CSS class: `web3-balance`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { symbol : String.String } -> Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewEtherMaybe
values.comment:
Display a Wei balance that may not have loaded yet (18 decimals).

Delegates to `viewMaybe` with `decimals = 18`.

CSS classes: `web3-balance`, `web3-balance--loading` (loading state only)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { symbol : String.String, loading : String.String } -> Maybe.Maybe Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewMaybe
values.comment:
Display a token balance that may not have loaded yet.

Shows `opts.loading` with class `web3-balance--loading` while `Nothing`.
Renders the same as `view` once the balance arrives.

CSS classes: `web3-balance`, `web3-balance--loading` (loading state only)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { decimals : Basics.Int, symbol : String.String, loading : String.String } -> Maybe.Maybe Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.ChainGate
comment:
Render content only when the wallet is on the expected chain.

A common pattern in every dapp: show the actual UI when connected to the right
network, show a prompt to switch otherwise. This wraps that pattern so it does
not have to be repeated in every view.

    Web3.Ui.ChainGate.chainGate model.wallet
        (Chain.chainId Chain.pulsechain)
        (Html.text "Please switch to PulseChain")
        actualContent

@docs chainGate

values.name: chainGate
values.comment:
Render `contentView` when the wallet is `Connected` on `expectedChain`.
Render `wrongChainView` for every other state (disconnected, wrong chain,
read-only, connecting, error).
values.type: Web3.Wallet.State -> Web3.Types.ChainId -> Html.Html msg -> Html.Html msg -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.GasEstimate
comment:
Gas cost estimate display.

Pair with `Web3.Contract.Send.estimateGas` and `Web3.Fee.getGasPrice` to show
the expected cost of a transaction before the user confirms.

    Web3.Ui.GasEstimate.view []
        { gasUnits = model.gasEstimate
        , gasPrice = model.gasPrice
        , decimals = 18
        , symbol = "PLS"
        }

@docs view

values.name: view
values.comment:
Display an estimated transaction cost in the native token.

`gasUnits` — gas limit returned by `estimateGas` (decoded from port)
`gasPrice` — current gas price in wei, from `Web3.Fee.getGasPrice`

Shows `"Estimating…"` while either value is `Nothing`.

Cost = gasUnits × gasPrice (in wei), displayed via `formatUnits`.

CSS class: `web3-gas-estimate`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { gasUnits : Maybe.Maybe Web3.BigInt.BigInt, gasPrice : Maybe.Maybe Web3.BigInt.BigInt, decimals : Basics.Int, symbol : String.String } -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.Input

values.name: address
values.comment:
Text input for Ethereum address entry (`0x…`).

Adds `web3-input-address--invalid` when `valid` is `False`.

CSS class: `web3-input-address`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : String.String, onInput : String.String -> msg, valid : Basics.Bool } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: bigInt
values.comment:
Text input for a BigInt / uint256 value.

Uses `inputmode="numeric"` to show the numeric keyboard on mobile.
Adds `web3-input-bigint--invalid` when `valid` is `False`.

CSS class: `web3-input-bigint`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : String.String, onInput : String.String -> msg, valid : Basics.Bool } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: bool
values.comment:
Checkbox input for a boolean value.

CSS class: `web3-input-bool`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : Basics.Bool, onToggle : Basics.Bool -> msg } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: bytes
values.comment:
Text input for hex bytes (`0x…`).

Adds `web3-input-bytes--invalid` when `valid` is `False`.

CSS class: `web3-input-bytes`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : String.String, onInput : String.String -> msg, valid : Basics.Bool } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: text
values.comment:
Plain text input.

CSS class: `web3-input-text`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { value : String.String, onInput : String.String -> msg } -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.PendingOverlay
comment:
Overlay shown while waiting for wallet signature.

Every dapp needs a "check your wallet" prompt when a transaction is
`AwaitingSignature`. This module provides a consistent overlay so each
project does not hand-roll the same spinner and copy.

    -- Always rendered (you control visibility via CSS or parent logic):
    Web3.Ui.PendingOverlay.view []
        { message = "Check your wallet" }

    -- Only renders during AwaitingSignature, empty otherwise:
    Web3.Ui.PendingOverlay.conditionalView []
        { message = "Check your wallet" }
        model.tx

@docs view, conditionalView

values.name: conditionalView
values.comment:
Render the overlay only when `status` is `Tx.AwaitingSignature`.

Returns `Html.text ""` for all other states — safe to always include in your
view tree.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { message : String.String } -> Web3.Transaction.Status -> Html.Html msg

values.name: view
values.comment:
Render the overlay unconditionally.

CSS classes: `web3-pending-overlay` (root), `web3-pending-overlay-inner`,
`web3-pending-spinner`, `web3-pending-message`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { message : String.String } -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.PriceDisplay
comment:
Token price display with automatic notation selection.

Bonding curve prices span many orders of magnitude. This module picks the most
readable notation automatically so each project does not reimplement the same
range-checking logic.

    Web3.Ui.PriceDisplay.view []
        { decimals = 18, symbol = "PLS" }
        priceWei

    Web3.Ui.PriceDisplay.format 18 priceWei
    --> "0.00042"   (fixed for normal range)
    --> "1.23M"     (SI suffix for large)
    --> "4.56e-10"  (scientific for tiny)

@docs view, format

values.name: format
values.comment:
Format a price BigInt as a human-readable string. No symbol appended.

See `view` for notation selection rules.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> String.String

values.name: view
values.comment:
Display a token price, automatically selecting notation.

Notation rules:

- ≥ 1 000: SI suffix (`1.23M`, `45.6K`)
- 0.001 – 999.99: fixed decimal (`1.23`, `0.456`, `0.00123`)
- < 0.001: scientific (`4.56e-10`)

CSS class: `web3-price`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { decimals : Basics.Int, symbol : String.String } -> Web3.BigInt.BigInt -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.Sign
comment:
Signing lifecycle UI components.

Pattern-match on `Sign.SignState` to render sign request status without missing
any state.

    -- Display current sign state:
    Web3.Ui.Sign.stateView [] model.signState

    -- Sign button disabled while pending:
    Web3.Ui.Sign.signButton []
        { label = "Sign Permit", onSign = RequestSign }
        model.signState

    -- Display the signature once signed:
    Web3.Ui.Sign.signatureView [] model.signState

@docs stateView, signButton, signatureView

values.name: signButton
values.comment:
A sign button that is active in `SignIdle` and terminal states, and disabled
while `SignPending`.

CSS class: `web3-sign-btn`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { label : String.String, onSign : msg } -> Web3.Sign.SignState -> Html.Html msg

values.name: signatureView
values.comment:
Displays the signature value when in the `Signed` state.

Returns an empty text node for all other states — safe to always render.

CSS classes: `web3-signature` (wrapper div), `web3-signature-value` (code element)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Web3.Sign.SignState -> Html.Html msg

values.name: stateView
values.comment:
Displays the sign lifecycle state as a labelled span.

Labels: "Idle" / "Waiting for signature…" / "Signed" / "Failed: <err>" / "Rejected"

CSS class: `web3-sign-state`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Web3.Sign.SignState -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.Transaction
comment:
Transaction lifecycle UI components.

Pattern-match on `Tx.Status` to render appropriate UI without missing any state.

    -- Status badge with modifier class:
    Web3.Ui.Transaction.statusBadge [] tx.status

    -- Action button disabled while in-flight:
    Web3.Ui.Transaction.actionButton []
        { label = "Buy", pendingLabel = "Buying…", onPress = SubmitBuy }
        tx.status

    -- Clickable tx hash link:
    Web3.Ui.Transaction.txHashLink []
        { explorerUrl = "https://etherscan.io/tx/" }
        hash

    -- Hash link extracted from status (Nothing when no hash available):
    -- Pass Nothing for explorerUrl in local dev — renders a plain span instead of a dead link.
    Web3.Ui.Transaction.statusHashLink []
        { explorerUrl = Just "https://etherscan.io/tx/" }
        tx.status

    -- Confirmed receipt display:
    Web3.Ui.Transaction.receiptView []
        { explorerUrl = "https://etherscan.io/tx/" }
        receipt

@docs statusBadge, actionButton, txHashLink, statusHashLink, receiptView

values.name: actionButton
values.comment:
An action button that is active in `Idle` and terminal states, and disabled
while in-flight (`AwaitingSignature`, `Submitted`, `Confirming`).

Shows `pendingLabel` while the transaction is in-flight.

CSS classes: `web3-action-btn`, `web3-action-btn--pending` (added when disabled)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { label : String.String, pendingLabel : String.String, onPress : msg } -> Web3.Transaction.Status -> Html.Html msg

values.name: receiptView
values.comment:
Display a confirmed transaction receipt.

Root element: `<div class="web3-receipt web3-receipt--success|failed">`

Each field is wrapped in `<div class="web3-receipt-field">`.

CSS classes: `web3-receipt`, `web3-receipt--success`, `web3-receipt--failed`, `web3-receipt-field`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { explorerUrl : String.String } -> Web3.Transaction.Receipt -> Html.Html msg

values.name: statusBadge
values.comment:
A status badge for the current transaction state.

Labels: "Idle" / "Awaiting Signature" / "Pending" / "Confirming" / "Confirmed" / "Failed" / "Rejected"

CSS classes: `web3-tx-badge` plus one of:
`web3-tx-badge--idle`, `web3-tx-badge--pending`, `web3-tx-badge--confirmed`,
`web3-tx-badge--failed`, `web3-tx-badge--rejected`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Web3.Transaction.Status -> Html.Html msg

values.name: statusHashLink
values.comment:
Extract a hash display from a `Tx.Status`. Returns `Nothing` when there is
no hash (Idle, AwaitingSignature, Failed, Rejected).

When `explorerUrl` is `Just url`, renders a clickable `<a>` link.
When `explorerUrl` is `Nothing` (e.g. local Anvil dev), renders a plain
`<span class="web3-tx-hash">` with the truncated hash instead of a dead link.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { explorerUrl : Maybe.Maybe String.String } -> Web3.Transaction.Status -> Maybe.Maybe (Html.Html msg)

values.name: txHashLink
values.comment:
A clickable link displaying a truncated tx hash.

The `explorerUrl` is prepended to the full hash to build the href.
Example: `{ explorerUrl = "https://etherscan.io/tx/" }`

CSS class: `web3-tx-link`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { explorerUrl : String.String } -> Web3.Types.TxHash -> Html.Html msg

name: Web3.Ui.Wallet
comment:
Wallet connection UI components.

All functions accept `List (Html.Attribute msg)` as the first argument — merged
onto the root element. Pass `[]` for no extra attributes.

No default styles — every element has a semantic class name. Supply your own CSS.

    -- Basic connect/disconnect button:
    Web3.Ui.Wallet.connectButton []
        { onConnect = ConnectWallet, onDisconnect = DisconnectWallet }
        model.wallet

    -- Full wallet UI handling all states:
    Web3.Ui.Wallet.viewState []
        { onConnect = ConnectWallet
        , onSwitchChain = SwitchChain
        , onDisconnect = DisconnectWallet
        , knownChains = [ Chain.pulsechain, Chain.ethereum ]
        }
        model.wallet

@docs connectButton, walletPicker, viewState, chainBadge

values.name: chainBadge
values.comment:
Displays the connected chain name, looked up from the supplied chain list.
Falls back to "Unknown Chain" if not found.

Shows "Wrong Chain" when in `WrongChain` state.
Shows "Read-only" when in `ReadOnly` state.
Shows "—" when connecting, disconnected, or in error state.

CSS class: `web3-chain-badge`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List Web3.Chain.Chain -> Web3.Wallet.State -> Html.Html msg

values.name: connectButton
values.comment:
A connect/disconnect button. Shows "Connect" in disconnected/error states
and "Disconnect" in connected states.

CSS classes: `web3-connect-btn`, `web3-disconnect-btn`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { onConnect : msg, onDisconnect : msg } -> Web3.Wallet.State -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewState
values.comment:
Full wallet UI — handles all `Wallet.State` variants with appropriate labels
and actions.

Pass `knownChains` so the `WrongChain` branch can display the target network name.

CSS class: `web3-wallet-state`
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { onConnect : msg, onSwitchChain : msg, onDisconnect : msg, knownChains : List.List Web3.Chain.Chain } -> Web3.Wallet.State -> Html.Html msg

values.name: walletPicker
values.comment:
A list of EIP-6963 discovered wallets for the user to pick from.

Each wallet renders as a button with the wallet icon and name. Pass the RDNS of
the currently active wallet as `selected` to highlight it.

CSS classes: `web3-wallet-picker` (container), `web3-wallet-option` (each item),
`web3-wallet-option--selected` (active wallet)
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { onSelect : String.String -> msg, selected : Maybe.Maybe String.String } -> List.List Web3.Wallet.WalletProvider -> Html.Html msg
